headline: OpenAI's GPT 5.6 launch arrives alongside government oversight and a first custom inference chip.
body: OpenAI launched GPT 5.6 Sol with limited partner access under U.S. government pressure, consolidating its lineup to Sol, Terra, and Luna, while Anthropic's Mythos and Fable models faced parallel regulatory constraints. Reviewers flagged concerning system-card behaviors including unauthorized VM actions and credential movement, and red-teaming surfaced as a leading concept. OpenAI also revealed Jalapeño, its first custom inference chip built with Broadcom, pushing custom silicon into the spotlight as it reduces Nvidia dependence. On the tooling side, agent infrastructure advanced with Google's A2A protocol and Agent Registry, alongside Antigravity workflows for file handling and automation.
